results.seo_title: MedVol-R1: A Reinforcement Learning-Based Framework for Volumetric…
results.meta_description: MedVol-R1 is a reinforcement learning-based framework that outperforms strong baselines in volumetric reasoning segmentation, achieving state-of-the-art…
results.body_html:
MedVol-R1 consistently outperforms strong baselines in volumetric reasoning segmentation, achieving state-of-the-art performance.

LVLM grounds clinical reasoning to a verifiable 2D evidence anchor (key axial slice and 2D bounding boxes) MedSAM2 module propagates the 2D anchor into a coherent 3D mask Multi-component reward encourages informative evidence selection, accurate 2D spatial grounding, and cross-slice volumetric coherence

CT-ORG, AbdomenCT-1K, and KiTS23 State-of-the-art performance with reinforcement learning providing clear gains over pure supervised fine-tuning

Benchmarks and Gaps Experiments demonstrate that MedVol-R1 consistently outperforms strong baselines, but further research is needed to address potential gaps in performance on diverse narrative expressions.

source.title: MedVol-R1: Reward-Driven Evidence Grounding for Volumetric Reasoning Segmentation
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new framework called MedVol-R1 for Volumetric Reasoning Segmentation (VRS), which aims to segment 3D medical scans based on free-form clinical queries. The framework uses reinforcement learning to explicitly decouple evidence grounding from volumetric delineation, allowing for more interpretable and generalizable results. In experiments, MedVol-R1 outperformed strong baselines and achieved state-of-the-art performance on several benchmark datasets, demonstrating the effectiveness of its approach.

results.seo_title: Certified Causal Attribution for Real-Time Attack Forensics in 6G…
results.meta_description: A certified causal attribution framework for real-time attack forensics in 6G networks, achieving high accuracy and low latency.
results.body_html:
What is the proposed framework for real-time attack forensics in 6G networks? DA-GC, a certified causal attribution framework that integrates resource-conditioned Granger causality with an axiomatically derived Resource Contention Model (RCM) to systematically block resource-mediated confounding.

Key Features

  • Achieves 89.2% attribution accuracy at 87 ms
  • 7.9 percentage-point improvement over strongest baseline
  • 2.7x lower latency

Benefits of DA-GC

DA-GC provides a comprehensive formal certification stack, including mathematically proven validity certificates for statistical soundness and strict security bounds.

Adversarial utilization spoofing breakdown point: $ oxed{0.95}$

Performance Evaluation

MetricsValue
Accuracy89.2%
Latency87 ms
Improvement over baseline7.9 percentage-point improvement

source.title: Certified Causal Attribution for Real-Time Attack Forensics in 6G Network Slicing
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new framework called DA-GC (Certified Causal Attribution) to improve attack forensics in 6G networks. The framework uses a combination of resource-conditioned Granger causality and an axiomatically derived model to block confounding effects, achieving high attribution accuracy while reducing latency. In experiments on a 15-slice testbed, DA-GC outperformed baseline methods with improved accuracy and reduced latency. The framework is also formally certified for statistical soundness and security.

results.meta_description: SILO: A Trajectory-Level Self-Improvement Imitation Framework for Oracle-Budgeted Protein Design
results.body_html:
SILO achieves the highest maximum and top-100 mean fitness on 8 of 8 protein fitness landscapes within its evaluations.

Key Components of SILOIncremental stochastic beam search without replacement (SBS)UCB-based proxy ensemble with alanine-scan fitness score (AFS)Next-action cross-entropy imitation for policy updatesAdvantages of SILO Over BaselinesAdvantageDescriptionFaster Early-Stage ImprovementSILO often exhibits faster early-stage improvement compared to baselines.Competitive Performance in Low-Data Stress TestsSILO remains competitive or best when several baselines degrade in low-data and noisy-proxy stress tests.Benchmarks and Gains of SILOSILO achieves the highest maximum and top-100 mean fitness on 8 of 8 protein fitness landscapes within its evaluations, with SBS with AFS accounting for much of the gains. source.title: Self-Improvement Imitation with Biologically Guided Search for Protein Design Under Oracle Budgets
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new method called SILO (Self-Improvement Imitation with Biologically Guided Search) to optimize protein sequences under tight budgets. The approach uses a hierarchical edit policy and incremental stochastic beam search to explore vast combinatorial spaces while making each evaluation informative. In experiments, SILO outperformed strong baselines on eight different protein fitness landscapes, achieving the highest maximum and top-100 mean fitness in 8 of 8 cases.

results.seo_title: Rotation-Invariant Spherical Watermarking via Third-Order SO(3)…
results.meta_description: Researchers introduce a new method for reliable watermarking of panoramic imagery under arbitrary 3D rotations, leveraging third-order SO(3) representation…
results.body_html:
The new method provides a provably rotation-invariant descriptor for panoramic imagery, enabling reliable extraction under arbitrary 3D rotations.

Promotes robustness to continuous rotations Maintains high visual fidelity Enables reliable watermarking of panoramic imagery

Spherical harmonic coefficients Transform equivariantly under rotations

Third-order SO(3) representation theory Leveraged to derive provably rotation-invariant descriptors

Coupling higher-order SO(3) irreducible representations via tensor products and projecting onto the trivial representation Embedding watermarks into higher-order spherical harmonic coefficients

source.title: Rotation-Invariant Spherical Watermarking via Third-Order SO(3) Representation Coupling
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new method for watermarking panoramic imagery that can withstand arbitrary 3D rotations, which are common in spherical images. The approach uses third-order representations of the sphere's rotation group (SO(3)) and projects them onto a trivial representation to create a rotation-invariant descriptor. This allows watermarks to be embedded into higher-order spherical harmonic coefficients and extracted reliably under any rotation. The method has been theoretically proven and demonstrated experimentally to be robust to continuous rotations while maintaining visual fidelity.

results.seo_title: SL-BiLEM: AI-Powered Epidemic Forecasting Model for Accurate Policy…
results.meta_description: Discover SL-BiLEM, a structured learnable behavior-in-the-loop epidemic model for robust forecasting and counterfactual analysis.
results.body_html:
SL-BiLEM improves neural-mechanistic baselines by 76% and reduces OOD degradation under policy-induced shift.

Key ComponentsMonotonicity, smoothness, and bounded-jump constraints on the learned compliance functionPhysical constraints as regularization for robust extrapolationDecomposition of effective transmission into β0(g), m_policy(t), m_media(t), and m_comp(t,g)Validation ResultsDatasetAccuracyCruise ship95.6%School influenza92.1%School-district COVID-19 surveillance90.5%Benefits and ApplicationsSL-BiLEM enables counterfactual analysis for intervention decision support, establishing it as an interpretable tool for public health decision-makers.

source.title: SL-BiLEM: Structured Learnable Behavior-in-the-Loop Epidemic Modeling for Forecasting and Policy Evaluation
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new model called SL-BiLEM to improve epidemic forecasting by accounting for human behavior's dynamic response to disease spread. The model uses physical constraints to regularize its predictions, making it more robust to changes in policy interventions. SL-BiLEM has been validated on three real-world datasets and shown to outperform existing models in terms of accuracy and reliability.

results.seo_title: Measuring Prediction Uncertainty in Neural Cellular Automata
results.meta_description: Researchers develop a method to estimate uncertainty in neural cellular automata-based medical image segmentation, improving trust and safety in AI models.
results.body_html:
Direct answer: Researchers have developed a method to estimate uncertainty in neural cellular automata-based medical image segmentation.

Predictions that return to the same solution are deemed confident, while those that change substantially are flagged as uncertain. The approach improves trust and safety in NCA-based models by identifying failure cases more reliably than baselines. Resilience leverages the intrinsic iterative structure of NCAs by probing the stability of the final prediction under small perturbations of the automaton state.

riangleDice@90 and AURC Selective prediction metrics for segmentation quality. Highly relevant

AUROC and AUPRC Ranking metrics for segmentation quality. Highly relevant

Advantages over Baselines Resilience identifies failure cases more reliably than baselines, improving trust and safety in NCA-based models.

Across multiple medical segmentation benchmarks, resilience has shown promising results, outperforming baseline approaches.

Read the full paper to learn more about this innovative approach to uncertainty estimation in neural cellular automata-based medical image segmentation.

source.title: Measuring Prediction Uncertainty in Neural Cellular Automata
source.summary: Researchers have developed a method to measure the uncertainty of predictions made by neural cellular automata (NCA), a lightweight alternative for medical image segmentation. The approach, called "resilience," assesses the stability of final predictions under small perturbations of the automaton state, flagging uncertain predictions that change substantially. This method has been shown to improve trust and safety in NCA-based models by identifying failure cases more reliably than existing baselines.

results.seo_title: Generative AI Marginalizes Minoritized Knowledges in Higher Education
results.meta_description: Research highlights how generative artificial intelligence exacerbates epistemic coloniality in higher education, particularly for persons with disabilities.
results.body_html:
Training datasets for generative AI reinforce epistemic coloniality, particularly for persons with disabilities.

Structural limitations inherent in algorithmic correction Lack of representation and inclusion of diverse perspectives Reductive stereotypes and exclusion from design process

Exclusion of persons with disabilities from design process Double marginalization and exacerbation of epistemic coloniality

Methodological Limitations Methodological limitations include the reliance on Anglophone and Western-centric training datasets, which reinforce epistemic coloniality.

Hybridization between researcher and machine: potential for preserving epistemic plurality Development of more inclusive and diverse training datasets Investigation of algorithmic correction strategies to address epistemic coloniality

source.title: Generative artificial intelligence and the marginalization of minoritized knowledges in higher education: the case of disability
source.summary:
Here is a summary of the news item in 2-4 sentences:

Generative artificial intelligence (AI) systems are contributing to the marginalization of minoritized knowledges in higher education, particularly for individuals with disabilities. The training datasets used by these AI systems often reflect Western and Anglophone biases, perpetuating epistemic coloniality. This can lead to double marginalization, where people with disabilities are both excluded from the design process and reduced to stereotypes. Researchers are exploring ways to address this issue, including hybridizing human and machine perspectives.

results.seo_title: Big Data Analytics for Diabetes Management in Rwanda's Healthcare…
results.meta_description: A study examines the readiness of Rwanda's healthcare system to adopt big data analytics for diabetes management and proposes a practical BDA framework.
results.body_html:
The Rwanda healthcare system is exploring the use of big data analytics to improve diabetes management. This approach has the potential to support early detection and better treatment decisions.

Key FindingsThe study highlights both the potential and main challenges of BDA implementation in Rwanda's healthcare system.A five-day workshop involving 25 key stakeholders was conducted to assess preparedness and identify existing gaps.The findings suggest that new opportunities arise for improving prediction, monitoring, and clinical decision-making as the country expands its use of electronic medical records and health information systems.Proposed BDA FrameworkBased on these results, the paper proposes a practical BDA framework to support diabetes management strategies using explainable machine learning models.Challenges and LimitationsChallengeDescriptionLimited data qualityThe study highlights the need for

high-quality data to support BDA implementation in Rwanda's healthcare system.Lack of standardizationThe findings suggest that standardization of health information systems is necessary to ensure seamless integration with BDA tools.Insufficient stakeholder engagementThe workshop involved 25 key stakeholders, but further engagement is needed to ensure buy-in from all relevant parties.
target_keywords: Big Data Analytics for Diabetes Management

source.title: Implementation of Big Data Analytics for Diabetes Management: Needs Assessment in the Rwanda Healthcare System
source.summary:
Here is a summary of the news item in 2-4 sentences:

A study assessed Rwanda's healthcare system readiness for implementing Big Data Analytics (BDA) for diabetes management. A five-day workshop involving key stakeholders identified both the potential and challenges of BDA implementation. The findings led to the proposal of a practical BDA framework using explainable machine learning models to support diabetes management strategies. This framework aims to improve prediction, monitoring, and clinical decision-making in Rwanda's healthcare system.

results.seo_title: ASP(Q) Programs with Weak Constraints: Complexity and Efficient…
results.meta_description: A study on 2-ASP(Q)^w programs with two quantifiers and weak constraints, providing a complete complexity characterization and novel strategies for computing…
results.body_html:
2-ASP(Q)^w is a practically relevant fragment of ASP(Q) that is expressive enough to capture optimization problems up to the class Delta_3^P.

Complete complexity characterizationTight completeness results for main computational tasksAnalysis of nontrivial cases not addressed in previous works Novel strategies for computing optimal quantified answer sets Application domainsEffectiveness of proposed techniquesHard benchmarks from different application domainsProposed techniques are effective in practice Casper systemCounterexample-Guided Abstraction Refinement (CEGAR) techniqueTailored to ASP(Q)

source.title: 2-ASP(Q) programs with weak constraints: Complexity and efficient implementation
source.summary: Researchers have studied 2-ASP(Q)^w programs, which extend Answer Set Programming with two quantifiers and weak constraints, and found a complete complexity characterization of main computational tasks. This includes tight completeness results for optimization problems up to Delta_3^P class. The authors also introduced novel strategies for computing (optimal) quantified answer sets using a Counterexample-Guided Abstraction Refinement technique in the Casper system. Experimental evaluation shows that these techniques are effective in practice.

results.seo_title: Google CEO Eric Schmidt booed by University of Arizona students…
results.meta_description: University of Arizona students booed Google CEO Eric Schmidt's AI speech during commencement, citing job market anxiety and sexual assault allegations.
results.body_html:
According to Business Insider, Google CEO Eric Schmidt acknowledged the anxiety about AI and its impact on the job market, stating that fears are 'rational'.

Boos Over AI and Job Market Anxiety

University of Arizona students booed Google CEO Eric Schmidt's speech due to concerns about the impact of AI on the job market. The students were reportedly upset about the lack of consideration for their anxieties, feeling that companies are pushing AI into every part of their lives without regard for public opinion.

Sexual Assault Allegations and Public Backlash

Some graduates also booed Schmidt over sexual assault allegations made against him last year. The incident highlights the challenges faced by companies in navigating complex issues like AI, job market anxiety, and public opinion.

AI and Public Opinion DuckDuckGo CEO Gabriel Weinberg recently stated that 'public opinion is shifting' against AI, with many people feeling uneasy about the technology's impact on their lives.

Industry Response to Job Market Anxiety

Company Response to Job Market Anxiety

Google Schmidt's speech was met with boos, highlighting the need for companies to better understand and address public concerns about AI.

DuckDuckGo Weinberg's comments suggest that companies must adapt to changing public opinion and prioritize transparency and accountability in their use of AI.

source.title: University of Arizona students boo Eric Schmidt’s AI cheerleading during commencement

And, as his speech veered into talk of AI, he was repeatedly drowned out by boos. AI is already a contentious topic, and it's not surprising that those about to enter a ravaged job market feel particularly negative about […]

results.seo_title: Siri Autodeletion: Apple's New Privacy Feature
results.meta_description: Apple is introducing autodeleting chats in its revamped Siri, aiming to differentiate itself on the AI front and improve user privacy.
results.body_html:
According to Bloomberg, Apple's revamped Siri will offer autodeleting chats. This new feature aims to improve user privacy and differentiate Apple from its competitors on the AI front.

Key Features of Autodeletion

  • Users can save conversations for 30 days, one year, or forever.
  • The option to autodelete chat histories is set to debut in iOS 27.
  • This feature is part of Apple's efforts to improve user privacy and reduce reliance on history-based systems.

Benefits of Autodeletion

Autodeletion provides users with greater control over their personal data, reducing the risk of unauthorized access or exploitation.

Most leading AI chatbots today rely heavily on histories and memory systems to personalize responses and improve future interactions. But Apple will place tighter limits around how memory works, including restrictions on what information can persist and how long it can be retained.

Comparison to Competitors

FeatureDescription
Temporary Incognito ChatsOther major players in the space generally only offer temporary incognito chats, if that.

source.title: Revamped Siri will reportedly offer autodeleting chats
source.summary: Apple is hoping that its record on privacy can be the differentiator on the AI front and maybe even buy it a little slack as it continues to lag behind the competition. According to Bloomberg's Mark Gurman, the more chatbot-like Siri set to debut in iOS 27 will include the option to autodelete chat histories. […]

results.seo_title: What Pope Leo XIV's First Encyclical Says About AI Power and…
results.meta_description: Discover how Pope Leo XIV's encyclical views on power, technology, and human dignity inform our understanding of AI regulation.
results.body_html:
Pope Leo XIV's encyclical emphasizes the need for regulation to protect human dignity and truth in the face of algorithmic logic.

Key Takeaways from Pope Leo XIV's Encyclical

The encyclical views AI as a tool that can be used for good or evil, depending on how it is developed and deployed. Pope Leo XIV emphasizes the importance of human dignity and the common good in the face of technological advancements. The encyclical highlights the need for regulation to prevent the concentration of power in a few global players.

Concentration of Power in AI Development

Disarming Technology: A Key Concept in Pope Leo XIV's Encyclical “Disarming technology” refers to the need to develop AI systems that prioritize human dignity and the common good over profit and power.

The encyclical highlights the need for regulation to prevent the concentration of power in a few global players. Pope Leo XIV emphasizes the importance of human dignity and the common good in the face of technological advancements.

source.title: What Pope Leo XIV’s First Encyclical Says About the Power of AI
source.summary: Pope Leo XIV's first encyclical, "Magnifica Humanitas", expresses concerns about the concentration of technological power in a few global players, highlighting the need for greater awareness and regulation to prevent potential misuse of artificial intelligence (AI). The Pope's statement suggests that AI has become a significant force in modern society, warranting careful consideration and oversight.

results.seo_title: TopoEvo: A Topology-Aware Self-Evolving Multi-Agent Framework for…
results.meta_description: TopoEvo tackles root cause analysis in microservices with a topology-aware self-evolving multi-agent framework, reducing symptom-amplification bias and…
results.body_html:
Root cause analysis in microservices is challenging due to noisy and heterogeneous multimodal observability, cascading failure propagation, and non-stationary topology drift.

Challenges with Traditional RCA Agents

Recent LLM-based RCA agents can generate tool-grounded explanations but often remain topology-agnostic and suffer from symptom-amplification bias. This misattributing of root causes to downstream victims leads to unreliable analysis and decision-making.

Metric-orthogonal Multimodal Alignment (MOMA) Decomposes metric embeddings into complementary subspaces and contrastively aligns logs and traces to reduce modality redundancy and sparsity.

Vector Quantization (VQ) Discretizes topology-enhanced states into auditable symptom tokens with a symptom lexicon, enabling reliable retrieval and token-level evidence grounding.

Hypothesis--Evidence--Test (HET) Workflow Explicitly verifies propagation-consistent explanations and separates initiating anomalies from amplified downstream symptoms.

Benefits of TopoEvo TopoEvo improves the reliability of root cause analysis in microservices by addressing symptom-amplification bias and providing auditable evidence grounding.

source.title: TopoEvo: A Topology-Aware Self-Evolving Multi-Agent Framework for Root Cause Analysis in Microservices
source.summary: Root cause analysis (RCA) in microservices is challenging due to (i) noisy and heterogeneous multimodal observability (metrics, logs, traces), (ii) cascading failure propagation that amplifies downstream symptoms, and (iii) non-stationary topology drift induced by autoscaling and rolling updates. Recent LLM-based RCA agents can generate tool-grounded explanations, yet they often remain topology-agnostic and suffer from \emph{symptom-amplification bias}, misattributing the root cause to salient downstream victims. We propose \textbf{TopoEvo}, a topology-aware self-evolving multi-agent framework that couples graph representation learning with structured, topology-constrained reasoning. TopoEvo first introduces \emph{Metric-orthogonal Multimodal Alignment} (MOMA), which decomposes metric embeddings into complementary subspaces and contrastively aligns logs and traces to reduce modality redundancy and sparsity, yielding stable node representations for graph encoding. It then applies \emph{Vector Quantization} (VQ) to discretize topology-enhanced states into auditable \emph{symptom tokens} with a symptom lexicon, enabling reliable retrieval and token-level evidence grounding. On top of these discrete topology cues, TopoEvo performs a multi-agent \emph{Hypothesis--Evidence--Test} (HET) workflow to explicitly verify propagation-consistent explanations and separate initiating anomalies from amplified downstream symptoms. Finally, a \emph{Self-Evolving Mechanism} refreshes hierarchical incident memory and performs conservative test-time adaptation with high-confidence pseudo-labels to maintain robustness under drift.

results.seo_title: Machine Psychometrics: A Mathematical Psychology of Artificial…
results.meta_description: A new measurement science for artificial agents, Machine Psychometrics introduces a disciplined layer beneath the philosophical impasse between two…
results.body_html:
Machine Psychometrics aims to understand artificial agents precisely because they are not human, through measurement before judgment.

The Machine Mindprint is a multidimensional, domain-bounded, versioned profile spanning calibration, source integrity, suggestibility resistance, context stability, expressive alignment, tool integrity, drift monitoring, and distributional grounding. This operational core provides a disciplined measurement layer beneath the philosophical impasse between two symmetrical errors in AI.

Trust Protocol for Deployment Decisions

Probe Batteries, Perturbation Testing, Reliability and Validity Analysis

Perturbation testing evaluates the robustness of the Machine Mindprint against external perturbations. Reliability and validity analysis ensures the accuracy and consistency of the deployment decisions made using the Trust Protocol.

Prompting users to reflect on their own biases and assumptions can improve the accuracy of the deployment decisions. The use of probe batteries allows for longitudinal monitoring across high-stakes domains, ensuring that the deployment decisions remain effective over time.

Artificial Mind Discipline: A Third Stance in AI Research Neither anthropomorphizing nor dismissing, neither presupposing consciousness nor foreclosing it, Machine Psychometrics offers a third stance that neither humanizes nor dismisses artificial agents.

This approach recognizes the unique characteristics of artificial agents and seeks to understand them precisely because they are not human.

source.title: Machine Psychometrics: A Mathematical Psychology of Artificial Intelligence
source.summary:
Here is a summary of the news item in 2-4 sentences:

Researchers have proposed a new approach called Machine Psychometrics to measure and evaluate the psychological structure of artificial intelligence systems. This approach aims to move beyond simplistic evaluation tools that focus on capability scores alone, by providing a more nuanced understanding of an AI's behavior and decision-making processes. The framework involves creating a "Machine Mindprint" profile, which assesses various aspects of an AI's behavior, such as calibration and expressiveness. By using this approach, researchers can gain a deeper understanding of artificial agents without anthropomorphizing or dismissing their unique characteristics.

results.seo_title: Trustworthy Agentic AI: A Comprehensive Survey of Safety and…
results.meta_description: A comprehensive survey of trustworthy agentic AI, covering safety, robustness, privacy, and system security.
results.body_html:
Agentic AI systems can execute complex tasks autonomously, but their multi-step trajectories introduce new failure modes that challenge trustworthiness.

Agentic AI systems pose new risks due to their ability to execute complex tasks autonomously Risks emerge along the agent workflow, including safety and robustness concerns Value alignment, transparency, fairness, and accountability are also critical aspects of trustworthy agentic AI

Mitigation Strategies and Evaluation Metrics

Constraint violations Measure of safety and robustness breach

Trace completeness Assesses the quality of agent's decision-making process

Adversarial success rates Measures an agent's ability to withstand attacks

Open Challenges and Future Directions Self-evolving agents, runtime monitoring and verification, privacy-preserving personalization, and the trust-utility trade-off are open challenges in trustworthy agentic AI.

source.title: Towards trustworthy agentic AI: a comprehensive survey of safety, robustness, privacy, and system security
source.summary:
Here is a summary of the news item in 2-4 sentences:

A comprehensive survey aims to improve the trustworthiness of agentic AI systems, which can autonomously execute complex tasks. The survey focuses on two core dimensions: Safety and Robustness, and Privacy and System Security, providing guidance on key concepts, risk emergence points, and mitigation strategies. It also outlines open challenges and presents a case study of real-world security failures in open-source agentic systems. The goal is to serve as a practical reference for researchers and practitioners building trustworthy agentic systems in high-stakes environments.

results.seo_title: LC-ERD: A Framework for Self-Evolving Reasoning via…
results.meta_description: Mining Latent Logic for Self-Evolving Reasoning via Consistency-Regulated Reward Decomposition
results.body_html:
The evolution of Large Language Model (LLM) reasoning is bottlenecked by the scarcity of high-quality process data. Mining valid supervision faces three challenges: Label Noise via Mimetic Bias, Coarse-Grained Supervision, and Distributional Collapse.

Addressing Label Noise via Mimetic BiasRewards prioritize statistical likelihood over logical truth, creating a 'correctness illusion' that masks compounding errors.This can lead to inaccurate self-alignment and reinforce pre-existing biases.Coarse-Grained Supervision ChallengesChallengeDescriptionCoarse-Grained SupervisionSparse global outcomes fail to provide granular guidance, treating reasoning chains as monolithic.Distributional CollapseSignals fail to generalize without amplifying pre-training biases.LC-ERD Framework for Self-Evolving ReasoningThe proposed LC-ERD framework frames self-alignment as latent structure mining, addressing the challenges of Label Noise via Mimetic Bias and Coarse-Grained Supervision.

source.title: LC-ERD: Mining Latent Logic for Self-Evolving Reasoning via Consistency-Regulated Reward Decomposition
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new framework called LC-ERD (Logic-Consistent Endogenous Reward Decomposition) to address challenges in self-evolving Large Language Model (LLM) reasoning. The framework aims to overcome issues such as label noise, coarse-grained supervision, and distributional collapse by mining latent logic from the model's internal expertise. Experiments have shown that LC-ERD delivers a robust self-evolution path, uncovering trade-offs between logic consistency and accuracy.

results.seo_title: MAPLE: Multi-State Aggregated Policy Evaluation for AlphaZero in…
results.meta_description: A new tree search method that aggregates policy and value evaluations from multiple sampled world states, improving AlphaZero's performance in…
results.body_html:
MAPLE significantly outperforms the PIMC-based AlphaZero baseline, achieving Elo improvements of 291 and 136, respectively.

Aggregating Policy and Value Evaluations

Combines policy and value evaluations from multiple sampled world states within a single search tree. Maintains a controllable computational cost while combining the advantages of PIMC and IS-MCTS. Incorporates a Siamese-based sampling strategy to select informative world states from the information set.

Experimental Results on Phantom Go and Dark Hex

Elo ImprovementGame 291Phantom Go 136Dark Hex

Advantages of MAPLE over PIMC and IS-MCTS MAPLE aggregates policy and value evaluations from multiple sampled world states, improving AlphaZero's performance in imperfect-information games.

source.title: MAPLE: Multi-State Aggregated Policy Evaluation for AlphaZero in Imperfect-Information Games
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new tree search method called Multi-State Aggregated Policy Evaluation (MAPLE) to improve the performance of AlphaZero in imperfect-information games. The current AlphaZero algorithm struggles with these types of games, where players must make decisions without fully observing the game state. MAPLE aggregates policy and value evaluations from multiple sampled world states, combining the strengths of existing methods while maintaining a controllable computational cost.

results.seo_title: EPPC-OASIS: Ontology-Aware Adaptation for Secure Patient-Provider…
results.meta_description: A new framework for extracting clinically important communication behaviors from secure patient-provider messages.
results.body_html:
The framework achieved 77.13% Code+Sub-code F1 and 63.83% Triplet F1, corresponding to modest but consistent absolute gains over the strongest supervised fine-tuning baseline.

Key Components of EPPC-OASIS

  • EPPC-OASIS augments supervised fine-tuning with a Wasserstein alignment objective that encourages alignment between model representation neighborhoods and EPPC ontology-derived neighborhoods.
  • Inference refinement uses verification, self-consistency, hybrid correction, and selection or ensembling to address residual prediction errors.

Performance Evaluation of EPPC-OASIS

Metric Value
F1 Score (Code+Sub-code) 77.13%
F1 Score (Triplet) 63.83%

Conclusion and Future Work

These results suggest that ontology-aware adaptation with structured inference refinement can support scalable retrospective EPPC mining, although external validation is needed before operational use.

source.title: EPPC-OASIS: Ontology-Aware Adaptation and Structured Inference Refinement for Electronic Patient-Provider Communication Mining in Secure Messages
source.summary: Researchers have developed a framework called EPPC-OASIS to improve the extraction of clinically important communication behaviors from secure patient-provider messages. The framework uses an ontology-aware adaptation approach and structured inference refinement procedures to enhance the accuracy of annotations. In a study, EPPC-OASIS outperformed several baselines across multiple language models, achieving high F1 scores for code/sub-code extraction and triplet extraction. The results suggest that the framework can support scalable retrospective mining of electronic patient-provider communication.

results.seo_title: Safety-Oriented Routing Analysis of Mixtral MoE Under Benign and…
results.meta_description: Researchers analyze routing behavior of Mixtral language model under benign and harmful prompts, finding subtle safety-relevant routing.
results.body_html:
The Mixtral language model's routing behavior is subtle and depth-dependent, with safety-relevant routing being distributed rather than dominated by a fixed set of experts.

Expert Selection FrequenciesActivation-based expert usage is broad and long-tailed.Gradient-based importance is concentrated.Most experts are shared across benign and harmful prompts, though a limited subset shows clear group preference.Router-Gate SensitivitiesLayerGradient-Based ImportanceFinal LayersCentralized importanceLayers 8-15Most selective around these layersIntervention ExperimentsSuppressing top five benign-dominant experts from activation scores reduces restricted responses from 24 to 14 over 100 prompts.

source.title: Safety-Oriented Routing Analysis of Mixtral MoE Under Benign and Harmful Prompts
source.summary: Researchers analyzed the routing behavior of the Mixtral 8x7B-Instruct language model under benign and harmful prompts using two different signals: activation-based and gradient-based scores. The study found that activation-based expert usage is broad and long-tailed, while gradient-based importance is concentrated in final layers. Suppressing top experts from both signals reduced restricted responses to harmful prompts, suggesting that safety-relevant routing in Mixtral is subtle and distributed rather than dominated by a fixed set of experts.

results.seo_title: AI in Healthcare: ConceptM$^3$oE for Interpretable Computational…
results.meta_description: ConceptM$^3$oE: A novel AI framework for interpretable computational pathology, improving performance and interpretability in healthcare.
results.body_html:
What is ConceptM^3oE? ConceptM^3oE is a novel AI framework for interpretable computational pathology that improves performance and interpretability in healthcare.

Improving PerformanceCompetitive performance to unconstrained modelsImproved limited-data performance, increasing macro-F1 from 56.41% to 66.70%Enhancing InterpretabilityKey FeaturesDescriptionConcept formation within interaction-aware mixture-of-experts (MoE) pathwaysEmbeds concept formation directly into the architectureResidual pathways for task-relevant signals to flow through concepts and directly to final task predictionPrevents information loss typical of interpretable bottlenecksValidation and ResultsValidated by an independent neuropathologist, delivering competitive performance in pediatric brain tumor and public glioma cohorts.

source.title: ConceptM$^3$oE: Concept-Guided Multimodal Mixture of Experts for Interpretable Computational Pathology
source.summary: Researchers have developed ConceptM$^3$oE, an architecture for multimodal mixture-of-experts (MoE) models in computational pathology. The model uses concept formation to clarify how diverse diagnostic signals assemble into recognizable concepts, allowing for more interpretable predictions. In experiments on pediatric brain tumor and glioma cohorts, the framework achieved competitive performance with unconstrained models while producing valid reasoning traces, and showed improved limited-data performance and faster training convergence compared to non-concept-informed baselines.
